heading: Find Your MongoDB Atlas Account
body:
You can manage your Atlas account's settings, unlink your account
from your Google or GitHub account, and configure multi-factor
authentication for your Atlas account.

To find your MongoDB Atlas account:

The Profile Info page opens. From here, you can:

- Access the MongoDB Atlas login page.

- Access Support to view active support cases and
  speak to the Support team.

- Visit the [MongoDB University](https://learn.mongodb.com/)
  to take free courses to become a certified expert on MongoDB.

- Visit the [MongoDB Community Forums](https://community.mongodb.com/)
  to meet other MongoDB developers.

From the left-side navigation bar, you can also:

- Provide your personal information in Profile Info.

  - If you manage your account through your Google Account, visit
    [your Google Account](https://myaccount.google.com/) to manage
    your personal information.

  - If you manage your account through your GitHub Account, visit
    [your GitHub Account](https://github.com/settings/profile) to
    manage your personal information.

- Unlink your MongoDB Atlas account from
  [unlink these accounts](unlink-your-mongodb-account-from-google) or
  [unlink these accounts](unlink-your-mongodb-account-from-github) in
  Profile Info.

- [enable multi-factor authentication](atlas-enable-mfa) in Security.
